claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch and router system implements brownfield PRD readiness with cross_check evidence policy and read-only evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier).

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan returns execution_policy with default_mode: 'dry_run', writes_business_code: false, agent_instruction: 'read_only'.
evidence.why: Primary dispatch plan confirms default dry-run and read-only policy.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent instructions with 'Hard rules: - READ ONLY. Do not edit, create, delete, or format project files.'
evidence.why: Agent-level read-only enforcement in generated prompts.

evidence.summary: executionConfig sets ai.codex_sandbox = 'read-only', ai.codex_approval = 'never' for Codex, and ai.custom_sandbox = 'read-only' for custom provider.
evidence.why: Provider-level read-only configuration for both Codex and custom agents.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime checks execute AND allow flags before execution; returns dry_run or blocked status without authorization.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement prevents accidental evidence agent execution.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, and verifier with writes_code: false and result_schema: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA.
evidence.why: Agent protocol definitions confirm read-only nature and result schema.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandTriage classifies brownfield vs greenfield, assigns evidence_policy (cross_check for technical/high-cost risks), and determines route.
evidence.why: Triage logic shows when cross_check evidence policy is triggered.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks required slots, blockers, assumptions, and evidence agent completion (lines 584-598) before declaring prd_ready.
evidence.why: PRD readiness inspection validates evidence agents must complete.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceTasks creates explorer task by default, adds cross-checker and verifier for cross_check policy.
evidence.why: Task generation confirms cross_check agent sequence.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts when agents disagree (proceed vs block/clarify) and returns blocked status.
evidence.why: Conflict detection prevents averaging agent disagreements.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult converts provider output into evidence_result schema with confidence, evidence, assumptions, risks, missing, recommendation.
evidence.why: Result normalization ensures required evidence structure.

evidence.summary: outputDirFor places evidence results in .yolo/demand/evidence/<dispatch-id>/ with role-specific JSON outputs.
evidence.why: Confirms evidence output location and file organization.

evidence.summary: Tests verify buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan returns dry_run default and runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime requires explicit authorization.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms security model and authorization requirements.

evidence.summary: Tests verify explorer, cross-checker, and verifier execute in sequence and feed results into readiness inspection.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms cross-check flow and PRD readiness integration.

evidence.summary: Tests verify agent conflicts (explorer says proceed, cross-checker says block) result in readiness blockers and prd_ready: false.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms conflict detection prevents unverified PRD promotion.

evidence.summary: Tests verify codex dispatch sets codex_sandbox: 'read-only', codex_approval: 'never', and does not inherit claude default model.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms Codex provider read-only configuration.
Shows CLI integration point for evidence dispatch functionality.

assumptions: Evidence agents are invoked via spawnProviderPrompt provider adapter, but the actual provider adapter implementation file is not examined in this cross-check.
Provider agents (Claude, Codex, custom) are assumed to respect the 'READ ONLY' hard rule in prompts; no lower-level enforcement mechanism is visible.
Demand session state structure (.yolo/demand/<id>/session.json) is assumed to match the schema implied by loadSession and buildStatusSession functions.
Git operations in runtime.ts are assumed to succeed in git repositories; fallback behavior for non-git contexts is not examined here.
risks: If provider adapter (spawnProviderPrompt) does not properly inject read-only instructions or config, agents could execute with write permissions.
If provider agents ignore the prompt-level 'READ ONLY' instruction, they could modify project files during evidence gathering.
If evidence dispatch is bypassed or agents return fabricated evidence, PRD facts could be generated from unverified assumptions.
Missing end-to-end integration tests with real provider agents means the actual security boundary is not fully verified.
missing: Actual provider adapter implementation (spawnProviderPrompt from runtime/execution/provider-adapter.js) is not examined.
Real usage logs or state files from .yolo/demand/ directories showing evidence dispatch has been run in production.
Integration tests showing full end-to-end evidence dispatch with real (not mocked) provider agents.
Evidence that provider agents actually respect read-only instructions in practice.

result.notes: All 16 primary evidence claims from explorer are independently verified through direct file reads. Evidence dispatch and router implementation correctly implements brownfield PRD readiness with cross_check policy, read-only agent execution, and conflict detection. Code and test evidence is comprehensive and internally consistent. Missing verification of provider adapter implementation and real-world usage keeps recommendation at cross_check instead of proceed, but no contradictions or blocking issues found.
